text:
GYNECOLOGIC ONCOLOGY CLINIC — SURVEILLANCE / MAINTENANCE THERAPY VISIT

ATTENDING: [Attending Gynecologic Oncologist]
VISIT TYPE: Established patient, oncology surveillance

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Routine surveillance visit; 18 months into Zejula maintenance therapy.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Ms. Okonkwo is a 59-year-old postmenopausal woman of West African descent with a germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant and FIGO stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (HGSOC), HRD-positive by tumor testing. She presents today for her scheduled 18-month surveillance visit on niraparib (Zejula) maintenance.

Brief disease summary: Diagnosed approximately 18 months after initial presentation with stage IIIC HGSOC, she underwent primary debulking surgery (PDS) achieving R0 resection. Final surgical pathology confirmed HGSOC with a serous tubal intraepithelial carcinoma (STIC) component and nodal involvement. She received six cycles of carboplatin and paclitaxel (carbo/taxol) as first-line therapy. CA-125 nadir was 18 U/mL following C6. She had a radiographic complete response on CT imaging after cycle 3, which was maintained through end of chemotherapy. She transitioned to Zejula maintenance per standard of care for BRCA1-mutated HGSOC.

Maintenance therapy course: She started Zejula at 200 mg daily. Within the first few months she developed grade 3 thrombocytopenia requiring dose reduction to 100 mg daily; she has remained at 100 mg throughout. She has had recurrent mild thrombocytopenia in the grade 1-2 range, which has been the primary hematologic toxicity. She also developed grade 2 hypertension that required augmentation of her antihypertensive regimen — she is now on dual therapy and BP has been well controlled at recent checks. A CT at approximately 6 months of maintenance and again at 15 months showed no evidence of disease. CA-125 at the 15-month mark was 22 U/mL.

Interval history: Since her last visit (approximately 3 months ago), she reports no new abdominal or pelvic symptoms. No bloating, early satiety, or change in bowel habits. No new neurologic symptoms beyond her baseline. She continues to experience mild residual peripheral neuropathy in her feet, which she describes as a persistent numbness affecting the tips of her toes — unchanged from prior visits. She endorses mild fatigue that she attributes partly to Zejula and partly to her thyroid and sleep (she has mentioned this before — I do not believe she is on thyroid supplementation but will recheck). She has returned to part-time work as a secondary school teacher and states her energy is much improved from where it was during chemotherapy. She denies chest pain, dyspnea, palpitations, or lower extremity edema. No bone pain or headaches.

Family/genetic counseling update: Of note, she relayed today that one of her three adult daughters recently tested positive for the familial BRCA1 pathogenic variant through cascade genetic testing. She states this daughter has been referred to a high-risk breast and ovarian cancer surveillance program. Ms. Okonkwo expressed mixed emotions — relief that her daughter now has access to intensified screening, but understandable anxiety. She was counseled briefly today and will be reconnected with our genetic counseling team for further family discussion if needed.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Positive: Mild fatigue (stable, chronic), bilateral toe numbness (stable, grade 1 peripheral neuropathy), mild occasional headache (attributed to HTN — currently controlled).

Negative: No nausea/vomiting, no diarrhea, no constipation, no abdominal pain or distension, no dysuria, no vaginal bleeding or discharge, no shortness of breath, no cough, no new musculoskeletal complaints, no visual changes, no syncope.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY / SURGICAL HISTORY (brief)
- FIGO IIIC HGSOC, germline BRCA1+, HRD+ — as above
- Hypertension — on dual antihypertensive therapy (see medications)
- Remote appendectomy (no complications per history)
- Chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy, grade 1 at present

---

MEDICATIONS
- Niraparib (Zejula) 100 mg PO daily (dose-reduced from 200 mg)
- Lisinopril 10 mg PO daily
- Amlodipine 5 mg PO daily (added for BP control during Zejula maintenance)
- Ondansetron 4 mg PO PRN (rarely used)
- OTC: Vitamin D supplementation

ALLERGIES: NKDA

---

FAMILY HISTORY
- Mother: breast cancer, diagnosed age 45
- Maternal aunt: ovarian cancer
- One adult daughter: BRCA1 pathogenic variant carrier (recently confirmed)

---

SOCIAL HISTORY
Postmenopausal. G3P3. Non-smoker, no tobacco. Rare social alcohol. West African descent. Part-time secondary school teacher. Lives with spouse. Has three adult children.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION

General: Well-appearing woman in no acute distress. Alert and conversational. Hair fully regrown.
Vitals: BP 128/76 mmHg, HR 74 bpm, RR 16, Temp 98.4°F, SpO2 98% on room air. Weight stable.
ECOG PS: 1.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. PERRL. Oropharynx clear, no mucositis.
Neck: Supple. No cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy appreciated.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs.
Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No crackles or wheezing.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. No tenderness to palpation in all four quadrants. Midline surgical scar well-healed. No palpable masses or organomegaly. No ascites appreciated on exam.
Extremities: No lower extremity edema. No calf tenderness.
Neurologic: Oriented x3. Cranial nerves grossly intact. Decreased sensation to light touch at bilateral distal toes, symmetric — consistent with prior peripheral neuropathy, no worsening from last documented exam. Normal gait and station.
Pelvic: Deferred today per patient request; last full pelvic exam performed 3 months prior with no abnormalities noted.

---

LAB RESULTS (obtained today)

CA-125: 22 U/mL — within normal limits; stable from prior draw (15 U/mL at last visit was 22; this is essentially flat, no upward trend).

CBC:
  WBC: 5.6 K/uL (normal)
  Hemoglobin: 11.3 g/dL (mild, grade 1 anemia — stable, chronic)
  Platelets: 148 K/uL (low normal; grade 1 thrombocytopenia — stable, chronic)
  ANC: 2.9 K/uL (adequate)

CMP: Creatinine 0.9 mg/dL, BUN 14, Na 139, K 4.1, liver enzymes within normal limits.

Note: Mild normocytic anemia and borderline-low platelets are chronic and consistent with ongoing Zejula effect and possible prior platinum effect; trend is stable over many months. No acute cytopenias of concern.

---

IMAGING

Most recent CT abdomen/pelvis: Performed approximately 3 months prior (15-month maintenance timepoint) — no radiographic evidence of disease. CA-125 at that time was 22. Next surveillance CT planning — likely at ~21 months given stable picture.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. FIGO IIIC HGSOC, germline BRCA1+, HRD+ — currently on Zejula (niraparib) maintenance, 18 months in.
   - Patient is doing extremely well. No clinical, serologic, or radiographic evidence of disease recurrence. CA-125 stable at 22 U/mL (normal). This represents a sustained complete response now roughly 2+ years from diagnosis.
   - We discussed the ongoing SOLO-1 data supporting extended maintenance in BRCA-mutated HGSOC patients — the 5-year follow-up data continue to show meaningful PFS benefit, and there is no pre-specified stopping point for patients tolerating maintenance. Plan to continue Zejula 100 mg daily indefinitely at this time.
   - Will continue CA-125 and CBC monitoring every 3 months. Next CT surveillance in approximately 3 months (21-month mark), or sooner if symptoms arise.

2. Zejula-related thrombocytopenia — grade 1, chronic and stable at current dose of 100 mg.
   - Platelets 148K today — acceptable. No dose modification needed. Will continue to monitor each visit. Patient aware of signs/symptoms of bleeding.

3. Chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy — grade 1, stable.
   - No functional limitations currently. Patient able to work and ambulate without difficulty. Will continue to monitor. No pharmacologic intervention at this time.

4. Hypertension — well controlled on lisinopril + amlodipine.
   - BP 128/76 today. No changes to regimen. Will continue to monitor.

5. Mild anemia — grade 1, stable, likely multifactorial (prior platinum effect, Zejula).
   - Hgb 11.3. No intervention needed at this level. Will recheck with routine labs in 3 months.

6. Genetic counseling / family update.
   - Daughter confirmed BRCA1 positive; referred to high-risk program. Patient counseled briefly. She declined a formal genetic counseling referral for herself at this visit but will reconsider if other family questions arise. Social work/counseling available per patient preference.

7. Quality of life / functional status.
   - ECOG PS 1. Patient has returned to part-time teaching — this is a meaningful recovery milestone. Fatigue manageable. We discussed sleep hygiene and gentle physical activity. No formal referral needed at this time.

Return to clinic: 3 months. Labs (CA-125, CBC, CMP) to be drawn 5–7 days prior to visit. CT imaging to be scheduled in coordination with next visit.

Time spent: Approximately 35 minutes. Greater than 50% of visit time spent on counseling and care coordination.

[Electronically signed — Attending Gynecologic Oncologist]
